ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”

translated: Jesus and his disciples came to Caesarea Philippi. He asked his disciples, "Who do you say that I am, the Son of the Living God?"

text: Iyɔ siakpese sikɛlɛ siakɔ siwarã lalaa aka akɔdzɛ mama dzɛ lonyanyarĩ siɖo sɛ̃ sibua so iso. Maabo i ngɔmɔ ame mawe, ne ɔso ɔ̃ kawirikɔ̃ to kaanyanyarĩ kaɖo ɔ̃ karɔ̃kasɛkɔ̃. Ngbɔ tutuutu iase itã nɔme maturi ma lonyanyarĩ ngbe ne.” Mabe Nɖe Yesu Manyii Mɔmɔ?
translated: John's mother and the prophet said that the child was in his house. And they brought the child to his mother, and he was circumcised on the eighth day as it is customary. And they named the child the name John as the prophet had said. But why is Jesus called Messiah?

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.

translated: Mawɛ̃ spoke the word of God to the Siwu people, and they went to preach with Jesus Christ. Jesus' word is true; he said, "The word is in your heart; believe and live."

text: Ne kama ne, Yesu ɔrui ɔnyua ma ɔkɛlɛ Betania ɔmagɛ̃ ame ngbegɔ ɔsɛ ɔ̃arɛ. Yesu Ɔgbarĩ Kuɖedziri Kuwɛ̃.
translated: And it came to pass that Jesus came and went near Bethany, and Mary his sister anointed him with oil. Jesus was invited to the house of Lazarus and they were with him.
